decision: open a local, proof-backed Promise continuation recovery slice for first-party Promise.resolve, Promise.reject, then, and catch while keeping broad async, thenable, finally, and aggregate equivalence closed

targeted_surfaces.newly_admitted_scope: first-party PromiseLike receivers with recoverable fulfilled or rejected local state; one-arg fulfillment and two-arg rejection handlers
first-party PromiseLike receivers with recoverable rejected local state
first-party static global Promise.reject as a rejected Promise channel, without thenable assimilation

implemented_recovery.semantic_admission_delta: positive for focused first-party Promise continuation fixtures; unchanged on repository-local crates Promise runtime rows because crates has no JS/TS Promise continuation source rows
newly_supported_equivalences: Promise.resolve(1).then(x => Promise.resolve(x + 1)) equals Promise.resolve(1).then(x => x + 1), with one Promise boundary retained
Promise.reject(1).catch(e => e + 1) equals Promise.reject(1).then(undefined, e => e + 1), with the recovered result behind a Promise boundary
handler-returned Promise.reject(reason) stays in the rejected channel instead of becoming a fulfilled payload
new_hard_negative_checks: handler result with possible thenable stays opaque
possible thenable Promise.resolve argument stays opaque
explicit PromiseLike Promise.resolve argument stays opaque
recovered Promise value does not converge with sync payload
recovered fulfillment stays distinct from unrecovered rejection

covered: Promise-returned resolve flattening without sync erasure
catch and then(undefined, onRejected) convergence without rejection-channel erasure

next_work: price Promise receiver producer proof beyond Promise.resolve and Promise.reject, especially constructor-created promises and async-function returns
keep Promise.finally and aggregate combinators reporting-only until settlement and aggregate-channel obligations have hard negatives
measure the 68 previously unhinted then/catch receiver occurrences again after adding producer-specific diagnostics
